It can not be read as in previous versions due to .csv file being Archived as .gz format archive for compression of space. #### CDR In .csv columns | **#** | **Column** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | 1 | Date/Time Start (UTC) | Call start date and time in UTC | | 2 | Date/Time Connect (UTC) | Call connect date and time in UTC | | 3 | Date/Time End (UTC) | Call end date and time in UTC | | 4 | Status | Call status (e.g. Completed, Failed) | | 5 | Source | Source number | | 6 | Source Name | Caller name (CNAM) | | 7 | Destination DID | Destination DID number | | 8 | Duration (sec) | Call duration in seconds | | 9 | Attempt | Routing attempt number | | 10 | Disconnect Code | SIP disconnect code | | 11 | Response | SIP response text | | 12 | Disconnect Initiator | Party that initiated disconnect | | 13 | Voice IN Trunk | Voice IN trunk name | | 14 | Destination | Call destination URI | | 15 | Trunk Group | Trunk group name | | 16 | Capacity Group | Shared capacity group name | | 17 | Toll-free (USD) | Toll-free charge amount in USD | | 18 | PSTN (USD) | PSTN termination charge amount in USD | | 19 | Metered (USD) | Metered channels charge amount in USD | | 20 | CNAM IN (USD) | CNAM lookup charge amount in USD | | 21 | Total (USD) | Total charge amount in USD | | 22 | Call ID | Unique call identifier | #### CDR Out .csv columns | **#** | **Column** | **Description** | | --- | --- | --- | | 1 | Date/Time Start (UTC) | Call start date and time in UTC | | 2 | Date/Time Connect (UTC) | Call connect date and time in UTC | | 3 | Date/Time End (UTC) | Call end date and time in UTC | | 4 | Status | Call status (e.g. Completed, Failed) | | 5 | Source | Source number | | 6 | CLI | Caller line identification | | 7 | Destination Number | Destination DID number | | 8 | Call Duration (sec) | Call duration in seconds | | 9 | Billing Duration (sec) | Billed duration in seconds | | 10 | Disconnect Code | SIP disconnect code | | 11 | Disconnect Reason | Disconnect reason text | | 12 | Voice OUT Trunk | Voice OUT trunk name | | 13 | Destination Country | Destination country name | | 14 | Network | Destination network name | | 15 | Call Type | Call type (e.g. International) | | 16 | Rate (USD) | Rate per minute in USD | | 17 | Charged (USD) | Charged amount in USD | | 18 | P-Charge-Info | P-Charge-Info header value | | 19 | Call ID | Unique call identifier |

request.description: > **Attention** > Please note that the Inbound/Outbound CDR export is available for current + 2 last months. Creates a single Export. DIDWW performs deletion of Export in 1 month after completion. #### Request Body Scheme > `{` > > > **data** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > **type** (*string, required*): **exports** > > > > > **attributes** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > > > **callback_url** (*string, optional*) > > > > The HTTP or HTTPS endpoint to where events related to export will be delivered. > > > > See [Callback details](#callbacks-details) for information about *callback_url*. > > > > > > > **callback_method** (*string, optional*) > > > > The HTTP Method used for order events. **post**, **get** are supported methods. > > > > See [Callback details](#callbacks-details) for information about *callback_method*. > > > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string, optional*) > > > > Identifier for export in external system. > > > > > > > **export_type** (*string, required*): **cdr_in** > > > > Type of requested CDRs. Possible values are **cdr_in** and **cdr_out**. > > > > > > > **filters** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > Export Filters Object > > > > > > > > > **from** (*string, NOT nullable, required*) > > > > > Start datetime of the CDR timestamp range (greater than or equals). Format: `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. > > > > > > > > > **to** (*string, NOT nullable, required*) > > > > > End datetime of the CDR timestamp range (less than). Format: `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. > > > > > > > > > **did_number** (*string, NOT nullable, optional*) > > > > > Filters CDRs by DID number. > > > > > > > > `}` > > > > > > `}` > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Request Body Example ``` json { "data": { "type": "exports", "attributes": { "export_type": "cdr_in", "filters": { "from": "2026-03-01 00:00:00", "to": "2026-03-31 23:59:59", "did_number": "123456789" } } } } ```

request.description: > **Attention** > Please note that the Inbound/Outbound CDR export is available for current + 2 last months. Creates a single Export. DIDWW performs deletion of Export in 1 month after completion. #### Request Body Scheme > `{` > > > **data** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > **type** (*string, required*): **exports** > > > > > **attributes** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > > > **callback_url** (*string, optional*) > > > > The HTTP or HTTPS endpoint to where events related to export will be delivered. > > > > See [Callback details](#callback-details) for information about *callback_url*. > > > > > > > **callback_method** (*string, optional*) > > > > The HTTP Method used for order events. **post**, **get** are supported methods. > > > > See [Callback details](#callback-details) for information about *callback_method*. > > > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string, optional*) > > > > Identifier for export in external system. > > > > > > > **export_type** (*string, required*): **cdr_out** > > > > Type of requested CDRs. Possible values are **cdr_in** and **cdr_out**. > > > > > > > **filters** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > Export Filters Object > > > > > > > > > **from** (*string, NOT nullable, required*) > > > > > Start datetime of the CDR timestamp range (greater than or equals). Format: `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. > > > > > > > > > **to** (*string, NOT nullable, required*) > > > > > End datetime of the CDR timestamp range (less than). Format: `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`. > > > > > > > > > **voice_out_trunk.id** (*string, NOT nullable, optional*) > > > > > ID of voice out trunk > > > > > > > > `}` > > > > > > `}` > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Request Body Example ``` json { "data": { "type": "exports", "attributes": { "export_type": "cdr_out", "filters": { "from": "2026-03-01 00:00:00", "to": "2026-03-31 23:59:59", "voice_out_trunk.id": "457bf47d-446d-41cd-91c3-dfbda7bf0753" } } } } ```

request.description: Update the settings of a single Export owned by your account. #### Request Body Scheme > `{` > > > **data** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > **id** (*string, required*) > > > > > **type** (*string, required*): **exports** > > > > > **attributes** (*object, required*) `{` > > > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string, optional*) > > > > Identifier for Export in external system. > > > > > > `}` > > > > `}` > > `}` > #### Note > > Only `external_reference_id` attribute can be updated. #### Request Body Example ``` json { "data": { "id": "77ebb57f-7b01-4178-bc84-9d1a8d2ae850", "type": "exports", "attributes": { "external_reference_id": "test" } } } ```

item.description: DIDWW API v3 Export functionality allows to create and download non-realtime .csv files with Call Detail Records (CDRs). Deletion of created Export is done in 1 month after completion. > ##### **Note** > > Real-time CDR mechanism for repetitive CDRs data transfer is available [here](https://doc.didww.com/events-api/index.html#cdr-streaming). Supported methods: `GET`, `POST`.

item.name: Country Object
item.description: #### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **countries** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **name** (*string*) > > > Country name (for example United Kingdom). > > > > > **prefix** (*string*) > > > Country prefix (country calling code, for example 44). > > > > > **iso** (*string*) > > > Country ISO code (for example GB). > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **regions** (*object*): ***to-many*** > > > relationship to [Region Object](#region-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example: ``` json { "id": "e352699c-3764-415b-8946-dd470c1e0ed7", "type": "countries", "attributes": { "name": "United Kingdom", "prefix": "44", "iso": "GB" }, "relationships": { "regions": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/countries/e352699c-3764-415b-8946-dd470c1e0ed7/relationships/regions", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/countries/e352699c-3764-415b-8946-dd470c1e0ed7/regions" } } } } ```

item.name: Region Object
item.description: #### Model: > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **regions** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **name** (*string*) > > > Region name > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **country** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Country Object](#country-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "id": "8ce33ee2-73da-4baa-85a0-cd607d0e9733", "type": "regions", "attributes": { "name": "Alberta" }, "relationships": { "country": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/regions/8ce33ee2-73da-4baa-85a0-cd607d0e9733/relationships/country", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/regions/8ce33ee2-73da-4baa-85a0-cd607d0e9733/country" } } } } ```

item.description: #### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **cities** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **name** (*string*) > > > City name > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **country** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Country Object](#country-object) > > > > > **region** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Region Object](#region-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example: ``` json { "id": "cdf449bc-e3fb-42cc-bf31-b88f91e40de4", "type": "cities", "attributes": { "name": "London" }, "relationships": { "country": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/cities/cdf449bc-e3fb-42cc-bf31-b88f91e40de4/relationships/country", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/cities/cdf449bc-e3fb-42cc-bf31-b88f91e40de4/country" } }, "region": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/cities/cdf449bc-e3fb-42cc-bf31-b88f91e40de4/relationships/region", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/cities/cdf449bc-e3fb-42cc-bf31-b88f91e40de4/country" } } } } ```

item.name: DID Group Object
item.description: DID Group Object attributes and meta attributes. #### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **did_groups** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **area_name** (*string*) > > > DID Group area name. This will be the name of the city, or a designation applicable to the area code such as National. > > > > > **prefix** (*string*) > > > DID Group prefix (city or area calling code). > > > > > **features** (*Array\[string\]*) > > > Features available for the DID Group, including voice, sms and t38. A DID Group may have multiple features. > > > > > **is_metered** (*boolean*) > > > Defines if the DID Group supports metered services (per-minute billing). > > > > > **allow_additional_channels** (*boolean*) > > > Defines if channel capacity may be added to this DID Group. > > > > > **service_restrictions** (*string, nullable*) > > > The message with service restrictions for specific Country/Area. > > > > `}` > > > **meta** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **needs_registration** (*boolean*) > > > Defines if end-user registration is required for this DID Group. > > > > > **is_available** (*boolean*) > > > Defines if numbers in this DID Group are currently in stock. > > > > > **available_dids_enabled** (*boolean*) > > > Defines if the DID Group supports numbers selection feature. > > > > > **total_count** (*integer*) > > > Total count of available DIDs in the DID Group. > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **country** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Country Object](#country-object) > > > > > **city** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [City Object](#city-object) > > > > > **did_group_type** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [DID Group Type Object](#did-group-type-object) > > > > > **region** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Region Object](#region-object) > > > > > **stock_keeping_units** (*object*): ***to-many*** > > > relationship to [Stock Keeping Unit Object](#stock-keeping-unit-object) > > > > > **address_requirement** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Address Requirement Object](#address-requirement-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example: ``` json { "id": "dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd", "type": "did_groups", "attributes": { "prefix": "77", "features": [ "voice_in", "sms_in" ], "is_metered": false, "area_name": "National", "allow_additional_channels": true, "service_restrictions": "Restriction Message A" }, "relationships": { "country": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/relationships/country", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/country" } }, "city": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/relationships/city", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/city" } }, "did_group_type": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/relationships/did_group_type", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/did_group_type" } }, "region": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/relationships/region", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/region" } }, "stock_keeping_units": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/relationships/stock_keeping_units", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/stock_keeping_units" } }, "address_requirement": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/relationships/address_requirement", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_groups/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/address_requirement" } } }, "meta": { "needs_registration": false, "is_available": true, "available_dids_enabled": false, "total_count": 109 } } ```

item.name: Stock Keeping Unit Object
item.description: Unique identification ID of inventory unit. #### Model: > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **stock_keeping_units** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **setup_price** (*string*) > > > Price for Order creation. > > > > > **monthly_price** (*string*) > > > Monthly price for order renew. > > > > > **channels_included_count** (*integer*) > > > Included channels capacity for each DID. > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example: ``` json { "id": "1a86de53-3131-491f-9e4b-3392da45d441", "type": "stock_keeping_units", "attributes": { "setup_price": "0.0", "monthly_price": "0.8", "channels_included_count": 2 } } ```

item.name: Available DID Object
item.description: #### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **available_dids** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **number** (*string*) > > > DID Number > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **did_group** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [DID Group Object](#did-group-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "id": "5265f3a0-0446-46f1-ae1a-f09946e80a9a", "type": "available_dids", "attributes": { "number": "19254349408" }, "relationships": { "did_group": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/available_dids/5265f3a0-0446-46f1-ae1a-f09946e80a9a/relationships/did_group", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/available_dids/5265f3a0-0446-46f1-ae1a-f09946e80a9a/did_group" } } } } ```

item.name: DID Reservation Object
item.description: #### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **did_reservations** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **expires_at** (*DateTime\[string\]*) > > > Expiration date and time. > > > > > **created_at** (*DateTime\[string\]*) > > > Creation date and time. > > > > > **description** (*string*) > > > Description > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **available_did** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Available DID Object](#available-did-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "id": "dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd", "type": "did_reservations", "attributes": { "expires_at": "2018-03-15 12:34:56", "created_at": "2018-03-15 12:14:56", "description": "DIDWW" }, "relationships": { "available_did": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_reservations/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/relationships/available_did", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/did_reservations/dd13cdb2-48ab-4374-94b3-56e162a659cd/available_did" } } } } ```

item.name: Balance Object
item.description: JSONAPI object that represents the user’s balance and has following attributes. #### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **balances** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **balance** (*string*) > > > Prepaid balance > > > > > **credit** (*string*) > > > Available credit > > > > > **total_balance** (*string*) > > > The net balance (balance+credit). > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "data": { "id": "94441ce5-86fa-47bd-8ce7-b5e267d0603a", "type": "balances", "attributes": { "balance": "50.00", "credit": "10.00", "total_balance": "60.00" } } } ```

item.name: Order Object
Order Items
DID Order Item
item.description:
## DID Order Item

#### Model:

> `{`
> 
> > **type** (*string*): **did_order_items**
> 
> > **attributes** (*object*) `{`
> > 
> > > **qty** (*integer*):  
> > > Quantity of services.
> > 
> > > **nrc** (*string*):  
> > > One-time activation fee (Non Recurring Cost).
> > 
> > > **mrc** (*string*):  
> > > Ongoing monthly fees (Monthly Recurring Cost).
> > 
> > > **prorated_mrc** (*boolean*):  
> > > If true, MRC will be charged prorated amount for the services acquired in the middle of the billing cycle.  
> > > If false, MRC will be charged full amount for the full billing cycle.
> > 
> > > **billed_from** (*date*):  
> > > Billing cycle start date.
> > 
> > > **billed_to** (*date*):  
> > > Billing cycle end date.
> > 
> > > **did_group_id** (*string*):  
> > > DID Group ID.
> > 
> > `}`
> 
> `}`

#### Example:

``` json
{
    "type": "did_order_items",
    "attributes": {
        "qty": 1,
        "nrc": "5.00",
        "mrc": "5.00",
        "prorated_mrc": true,
        "billed_from": "2018-08-15",
        "billed_to": "2018-09-15",
        "did_group_id": "a7b2abcb-6251-475f-b6e0-fd9acf2579ef"
    }
}

```

item.name: Capacity Order Item
item.description:
## Capacity Order Item

#### Model:

> `{`
> 
> > **type** (*string*): **capacity_order_items**
> 
> > **attributes** (*object*) `{`
> > 
> > > **qty** (*integer*):  
> > > Quantity of services.
> > 
> > > **nrc** (*string*):  
> > > One-time activation fee (Non Recurring Cost).
> > 
> > > **mrc** (*string*):  
> > > Ongoing monthly fees (Monthly Recurring Cost).
> > 
> > > **prorated_mrc** (*boolean*):  
> > > If true, MRC will be charged prorated amount for the services acquired in the middle of the billing cycle. If false, MRC will be charged full amount for the full billing cycle.
> > 
> > > **billed_from** (*date*):  
> > > Billing cycle start date.
> > 
> > > **billed_to** (*date*):  
> > > Billing cycle end date.
> > 
> > > **capacity_pool_id** (*string*):  
> > > Capacity Pool ID.
> 
> > `}`
> 
> `}`

#### Example:

``` json
{
    "type": "capacity_order_items",
    "attributes": {
        "qty": 1,
        "nrc": "20.0",
        "mrc": "1.33",
        "prorated_mrc": true,
        "billed_from": "2018-08-15",
        "billed_to": "2018-09-15",
        "capacity_pool_id": "c5f87307-7c80-417c-9ec3-18e0241c4228"
    }
}

```

#### Model: > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **orders** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **reference** (*string*) > > > Order Reference number. > > > > > **amount** (*string*) > > > Total order amount. > > > > > **status** (*enum*): **pending**, **completed**, **canceled** > > > Status of the Order. > > > > > **description** (*string*): > > > Description of the Order. > > > > > **callback_url** (*string*): > > > The HTTP or HTTPS endpoint to where events related to order will be delivered. > > > > > **callback_method** (*string*): > > > The HTTP Method used for order events. **post**, **get** are supported methods. > > > > > **created_at** (*DateTime*): > > > Date and time of Order creation. > > > > > **items** (*Array*): > > > [Ordered items](#order-items) array. For emergency orders, each item includes `emergency_calling_service_id` — the UUID of the associated Emergency Calling Service. > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string*): > > > Identifier for Order in external system. > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example: ``` json { "id": "9eeaab6c-b758-41b8-af86-8978a86603a2", "type": "orders", "attributes": { "reference": "FZH-374899", "amount": "79.2", "status": "pending", "created_at": "2017-06-25T14:56:31.513Z", "description": "DID", "items": [{ "type": "did_order_items", "attributes": { "qty": 15, "nrc": "5.00", "mrc": "5.00", "prorated_mrc": true, "billed_from": "2018-08-15", "billed_to": "2018-09-15", "did_group_id": "d01704b0-6522-47d5-8865-3398c417ed1d" } }], "callback_url": null, "callback_method": null, "external_reference_id": null } } ``` #### Emergency Order Example: ``` json { "id": "45d5f9ed-ecdc-47d0-93f5-b713d69b2fab", "type": "orders", "attributes": { "reference": "KBD-726526", "amount": "3.0", "status": "completed", "created_at": "2026-04-07T00:00:03.879Z", "description": "Emergency", "items": [{ "type": "emergency_order_items", "attributes": { "qty": 4, "nrc": "0.0", "mrc": "0.75", "prorated_mrc": false, "billed_from": "2026-04-06", "billed_to": "2026-05-06", "emergency_calling_service_id": "95c1cfa9-ffe8-4cc9-a089-682c56b3bd74" } }], "callback_url": null, "callback_method": null, "external_reference_id": null } } ``` #### 400 Insufficient Funds Error: ``` json { "errors": [{ "title": "Insufficient balance", "detail": "Insufficient balance", "code": "400", "status": "400", "meta": { "available_balance": "1.9", "total_cost": "2.0" } }] } ```

item.name: DID Object
item.description: #### Model: > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **dids** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **blocked** (*boolean*) > > > Identifier for a blocked DID. Blocked DIDs are numbers that have expired, have been cancelled or have been suspended by DIDWW. > > > > > **capacity_limit** (*integer*) > > > The capacity limit (maximum number of simultaneous calls) for this DID. > > > > > **description** (*string*) > > > Custom DID description for customers reference. > > > > > **terminated** (*boolean*) > > > Identifier for terminated DIDs that will be removed from service at the end of the billing cycle. > > > > > **awaiting_registration** (*boolean*) > > > Identifier for a DID that is awaiting registration. > > > > > **number** (*string*) > > > The actual DID number in the format . > > > > > **expires_at** (*DateTime*) > > > DateTime when the DID expired or will expire. DateTime is in the ISO 8601 format "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'", where "SSS" are milliseconds and 'Z' denotes Zulu time (UTC). > > > > > **channels_included_count** (*integer*) > > > The number of channels included with this DID. > > > > > **created_at** (*DateTime*) > > > DID created at DateTime. > > > > > **pending_removal** (*boolean*) > > > Identifier for DIDs pending removal. > > > > > **dedicated_channels_count** (*integer*) > > > The number of channels from Capacity Pool. > > > > > **emergency_enabled** (*boolean*) > > > Indicates whether emergency calling is enabled for this DID. > > > > > **billing_cycles_count** (*integer, optional*) > > > The number of Billing Cycles that this DID Number will renew until expiration. > > > `null` value means that DID will be renewed infinitely. `0` value means that DID will not be renewed. `2` value means that DID will be renewed only twice. After each renew value will be decreased by 1 if it is set. Value can be set from `0` to `999`. > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **did_group** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [DID Group Object](#did-group-object) > > > > > **order** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Order Object](#order-object) > > > > > **voice_in_trunk** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Voice In Trunk Object](#voice-in-trunk-object) > > > > > **voice_in_trunk_group** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Voice In Trunk Group Object](#voice-in-trunk-group-object) > > > > > **capacity_pool** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Capacity Pool Object](#capacity-pool-object) > > > > > **shared_capacity_group** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Shared Capacity Group Object](#shared-capacity-group-object) > > > > > **address_verification** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Address Verification Object](#address-verification-object) > > > > > **emergency_calling_service** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Emergency Calling Service Object](#emergency-calling-service-object) > > > > > **emergency_verification** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Emergency Verification Object](#emergency-verification-object) > > > > > **identity** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Identity Object](#identity-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "id": "44957076-778a-4802-b60c-d22db0cda284", "type": "dids", "attributes": { "blocked": false, "capacity_limit": 1, "description": "string", "terminated": false, "awaiting_registration": false, "number": "437xxxxxxxxx", "expires_at": "2017-06-25T08:21:41.795Z", "channels_included_count": 2, "created_at": "2017-06-25T08:21:41.795Z", "pending_removal": false, "dedicated_channels_count": 0, "emergency_enabled": false }, "relationships": { "did_group": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/dids/44957076-778a-4802-b60c-d22db0cda284/relationships/did_group", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/dids/44957076-778a-4802-b60c-d22db0cda284/did_group" } }, "order": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/dids/44957076-778a-4802-b60c-d22db0cda284/relationships/order", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/dids/44957076-778a-4802-b60c-d22db0cda284/order" } }, "voice_in_trunk": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/dids/44957076-778a-4802-b60c-d22db0cda284/relationships/voice_in_trunk", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/dids/44957076-778a-4802-b60c-d22db0cda284/voice_in_trunk" } }, "voice_in_trunk_group": { "links": { "self": item.name: Voice In Trunk Object
SIP Configuration Object
item.description: #### Model: > `{` > > > **type** (*string*): ***sip_configurations*** > > SIP configuration complex object. > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > SIP configuration attributes object. > > > > > **username** (*string, NOT nullable*) > > > User part of R-URI in INVITE request. > > > You also may use “{DID}” pattern which will be replaced by called DID number in E164 format. > > > For example, you can set Username to “+{DID}”; if you wish to have it in +E164 format > > > > > **host** (*string, NOT nullable*) > > > Host part of R-URI in INVITE request. > > > > > **auth_user** (*string, NOT nullable*) > > > Optional authorization user for the SIP server. > > > > > **auth_password** (*string, NOT nullable*) > > > Optional authorization password for the SIP server. > > > > > **auth_from_user** (*string, NOT nullable*) > > > Specify user in a *from* field instead of CallerID (overrides CallerID). > > > Some equipment require *from*; to be equivalent to *Auth* user. > > > > > **auth_from_domain** (*string, NOT nullable*) > > > Sets default *from* domain in SIP messages. Some equipment may require specific *From* Domain. > > > > > **sst_refresh_method_id** (*enumerate\[integer\], NOT nullable*): **1**, **2**, **3** > > > SIP method which will be used for session update. > > > See [RFC 4028](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4028) for more details. Possible values: > > > 1 - Invite > > > 2 - Update > > > 3 - Update fallback Invite > > > > > **sip_timer_b** (*integer, NOT nullable*) > > > INVITE transaction timeout (Default 8000ms). > > > See [RFC 3261 Section 17.1.1.2](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3261#section-17.1.1.2) for more details. > > > > > **dns_srv_failover_timer** (*integer, NOT nullable*) > > > Invite transaction timeout for each of gateways with DNS SRV rerouting (Default: **2000ms**). > > > > > **rtp_ping** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > Use RTP PING when connecting a call. > > > After establishing the call, DIDWW will send empty RTP packet *RTP PING*. > > > It is necessary if both parties operate in Symmetric RTP / Comedia mode and expect the other party to start sending RTP first. > > > > > **rtp_timeout** (*integer, NOT nullable*) > > > Disconnect the call if the RTP packets do not arrive within the specified time. > > > > > **sst_min_timer** (*integer, NOT nullable*) > > > Minimal SIP Session timer value (Default 600 seconds). > > > See [RFC 4028](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4028) for more details. > > > > > **sst_max_timer** (*integer, NOT nullable*) > > > Maximal SIP Session timer value (Default 900 seconds).. > > > See [RFC 4028](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4028) for more details. > > > > > **sst_session_expires** (*integer, NOT nullable*) > > > Session-Expires header value. Optional, should be in range with *sst_min_timer* and *sst_max_timer*. > > > See [RFC 4028](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4028) for more details. > > > > > **port** (*integer, NOT nullable*) > > > Port part of R-URI in INVITE request (is not mandatory). > > > If port is null, SRV record will be resolved (or A record if SRV is unavailable). > > > > > **rx_dtmf_format_id** (*enumerate\[integer\], NOT nullable*): **1**, **2**, **3** > > > The method id for receiving DTMF signals from customers equipment. > > > Possible values: > > > 1 - RFC 2833 > > > 2 - SIP INFO application/dtmf-relay OR application/dtmf > > > 3 - RFC 2833 OR SIP INFO > > > > > **tx_dtmf_format_id** (*enumerate\[integer\], NOT nullable*): **1**, **2**, **3**, **4** > > > The method of sending DTMF signals to customers equipment. > > > Possible values: > > > 1 - Disable sending > > > 2 - RFC 2833 > > > 3 - SIP INFO application/dtmf-relay > > > 4 - SIP INFO application/dtmf > > > > > **force_symmetric_rtp** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > Forced to work in Symmetric RTP / COMEDIA mode. > > > > > **symmetric_rtp_ignore_rtcp** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > Avoid switching RTP session based on RTCP packet while working in Symmetric RTP / COMEDIA. > > > Only RTP packets will be considered. > > > > > **sst_enabled** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > Enable SIP Session timers customization. > > > SIP session timers are used to make sure that a session (dialog) is still alive, > > > even though there may have been a long time since the last in-dialog message. > > > If the other end is not responding, the dialog will be hung up automatically. > > > SIP session timers need to be supported by all end points for it to work. > > > It's a SIP extension, standardized by the IETF. > > > See [RFC 4028](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4028) for more details. > > > > > **sst_accept_501** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > Do not drop the call after receiving SIP 501 response for non-critical messages. > > > > > **auth_enabled** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > Enable authorization for the SIP server. > > > > > **resolve_ruri** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > Replace host part of the R-URI by resolved IP address. > > > > > **rerouting_disconnect_code_ids** (*array\[integer\], optional, NOT nullable*) > > > [Rerouting Disconnect Codes](#rerouting-disconnect-codes) > > > > > **codec_ids** (*array\[integer\], NOT nullable*) > > > [Codecs](#codecs) > > > > > **transport_protocol_id** (*enumerate\[integer\], NOT nullable*): **1**, **2**, **3** > > > The transport layer that will be responsible for the actual transmission of SIP requests and responses (1 - UDP, 2 - TCP 3 - TLS). > > > > > **max_transfers** (*integer, NOT nullable*) > > > Max count of the REFER requests. > > > > > **max_30x_redirects** (*integer, NOT nullable*) > > > Max count of 301/302 redirects. > > > > > **media_encryption_mode** (*enumerate[string], NOT nullable*): **disabled**, **srtp_sdes**, **srtp_dtls**, **zrtp** > > > Media encryption mode. > > > See [RFC4568 about SRTP SDES](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4568), [RFC5764 about SRTP DTLS](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5764), and [RFC6189 about ZRTP](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6189) for more details. > > > Possible values: > > > `disabled` - Disabled > > > `srtp_sdes` - SRTP SDES > > > `srtp_dtls` - SRTP DTLS > > > `zrtp` - ZRTP > > > > > **stir_shaken_mode** (*enumerate[string], NOT nullable*): **disabled**, **original**, **pai**, **original_pai**, **verstat**, > > > Stir/Shaken mode. > > > See [STIR/SHAKEN](https://doc.didww.com/services/did/stir-shaken.html#service-did-stir-shaken) for more details. > > > Possible Values: > > > `disabled` - Do not send identity > > > `original` - Transit Identity header as is > > > `pai` - Add PAI, P-Attestation-Indicator, P-Origination-ID > > > `original_pai` - Transit Identity Header as is + Add PAI, P-Attestation Indicator, P-Origination-ID > > > `verstat` - Add P-Stir-Verstat, P-Attestation-Indicator, P-Origination-ID > > > > > **allowed_rtp_ips** (*array[string], nullable*) > > > The allowed RTP IPs. Array from 0 to 10 items: IPv4 or IPv6, single or subnet. > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "type": "sip_configurations", "attributes": { "username": "username", "host": "example.com", "port": 5060, "codec_ids": [ 9, 7 ], "rx_dtmf_format_id": 1, "tx_dtmf_format_id": 1, "resolve_ruri": true, "auth_enabled": true, "auth_user": "username", "auth_password": "password", "auth_from_user": "Office", "auth_from_domain": "example.com", "sst_enabled": false, "sst_min_timer": 600, "sst_max_timer": 900, "sst_accept_501": true, "sip_timer_b": 8000, "dns_srv_failover_timer": 2000, "rtp_ping": false, "rtp_timeout": 30, "force_symmetric_rtp": false, "symmetric_rtp_ignore_rtcp": false, "rerouting_disconnect_code_ids": [ 58, 59 ], "sst_session_expires": null, "sst_refresh_method_id": 1, "transport_protocol_id": 2, "max_transfers": 0, "max_30x_redirects": 0, "media_encryption_mode": "disabled", "stir_shaken_mode": "disabled", "allowed_rtp_ips": null } } ```

item.name: PSTN configuration Object
item.description: #### Model: > `{` > > > **type** (*string*): ***pstn_configurations*** > > PSTN configuration complex object. > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > PSTN configuration attributes object. > > > > > **dst** (*string*) > > > Phone number’s. > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "type": "pstn_configurations", "attributes": { "dst": "1xxxxxxxxx" } } ```

item.name: Rerouting Disconnect Codes
item.description: | ID | Code | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | 56 | 400 | Bad Request | | 57 | 401 | Unauthorized | | 58 | 402 | Payment Required | | 59 | 403 | Forbidden | | 60 | 404 | Not Found | | 64 | 408 | Request Timeout | | 65 | 409 | Conflict | | 66 | 410 | Gone | | 67 | 412 | Conditional Request Failed | | 68 | 413 | Request Entity Too Large | | 69 | 414 | Request-URI Too Long | | 70 | 415 | Unsupported Media Type | | 71 | 416 | Unsupported URI Scheme | | 72 | 417 | Unknown Resource-Priority | | 73 | 420 | Bad Extension | | 74 | 421 | Extension Required | | 75 | 422 | Session Interval Too Small | | 76 | 423 | Interval Too Brief | | 77 | 424 | Bad Location Information | | 78 | 428 | Use Identity Header | | 79 | 429 | Provide Referrer Identity | | 80 | 433 | Anonymity Disallowed | | 81 | 436 | Bad Identity-Info | | 82 | 437 | Unsupported Certificate | | 83 | 438 | Invalid Identity Header | | 84 | 480 | Temporarily Unavailable | | 86 | 482 | Loop Detected | | 87 | 483 | Too Many Hops | | 88 | 484 | Address Incomplete | | 89 | 485 | Ambiguous | | 90 | 486 | Busy Here | | 91 | 487 | Request Terminated | | 92 | 488 | Not Acceptable Here | | 96 | 494 | Security Agreement Required | | 97 | 500 | Server Internal Error | | 98 | 501 | Not Implemented | | 99 | 502 | Bad Gateway | | 100 | 503 | Service Unavailable | | 101 | 504 | Server Time-out | | 102 | 505 | Version Not Supported | | 103 | 513 | Message Too Large | | 104 | 580 | Precondition Failure | | 105 | 600 | Busy Everywhere | | 106 | 603 | Decline | | 107 | 604 | Does Not Exist Anywhere | | 108 | 606 | Not Acceptable | | 1505 | | Ringing timeout |

#### Model: > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > Unique ID identifier of Trunk. > > > **type** (*string*): **voice_in_trunks** > > Trunks > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > Trunk configuration complex object. > > > > > **priority** (*integer*) > > > The priority of this target host. DIDWW will attempt to contact the target trunk with the lowest-numbered priority; target trunk with the same priority will be tried in an order defined by the weight field. The range is 0-65535. See [RFC 2782](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2782) for more details. > > > > > **weight** (*integer*) > > > A trunk selection mechanism. The weight field specifies a relative weight for entries with the same priority. Larger weights will be given a proportionately higher probability of being selected. The range of this number is 0-65535. In the presence of records containing weights greater than 0, records with weight 0 will have a very small chance of being selected. See [RFC 2782](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2782) for more details. > > > > > **capacity_limit** (*integer*) > > > Maximum number of simultaneous calls for the trunk. > > > > > **ringing_timeout** (*integer*) > > > After which it will be end transaction with internal disconnect code Ringing timeout if the call was not connected. > > > > > **name** (*string*) > > > Friendly name of the trunk. > > > > > **cli_format** (*string*): ***RAW***, ***E164***, ***Local*** > > > *RAW* - Do not alter CLI (default). > > > *E164* - Attempt to convert CLI to E.164 format. > > > *Local* - Attempt to convert CLI to Localized format. > > > > > > *CLI format conversion may not work correctly for phone calls originating from outside the country of that specific DID.* > > > > > **cli_prefix** (*string*) > > > You may prefix the CLI with an optional `+` sign followed by up to 6 characters, including digits and `#`. > > > > > **description** (*string*) > > > Optional description of the trunk. > > > > > **created_at** (*DateTime*) > > > Trunk created at DateTime. > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string*) > > > Identifier for Voice In Trunk in external system. > > > > > **configuration** (*enumerated\[object\]*) > > > One of [sip_configurations](#sip-configuration-object), [pstn_configurations](#pstn-configuration-object) > > > > > > For **sip_configurations**, additional attributes are available: > > > > > > > **network_protocol_priority** (*enumerate\[string\]*): **force_ipv4**, **force_ipv6**, **any**, **prefer_ipv4**, **prefer_ipv6** > > > > *force_ipv4* - Force IPv4 for SIP signaling. > > > > *force_ipv6* - Force IPv6 for SIP signaling. > > > > *any* - Use any available protocol. > > > > *prefer_ipv4* - Prefer IPv4, fall back to IPv6. > > > > *prefer_ipv6* - Prefer IPv6, fall back to IPv4. > > > > > > > **enabled_sip_registration** (*boolean*) > > > > Enables SIP registration support on the trunk. When enabled, `host` must be blank and `use_did_in_ruri` must be enabled. > > > > > > > **use_did_in_ruri** (*boolean*) > > > > Use DID number in the Request-URI of outgoing SIP requests. Requires `enabled_sip_registration` to be enabled. > > > > > > > **diversion_relay_policy** (*enumerate\[string\]*): **none**, **as_is**, **sip**, **tel** > > > > *none* - Do not relay Diversion header. > > > > *as_is* - Relay Diversion header as is. > > > > *sip* - Relay Diversion header as SIP URI. > > > > *tel* - Relay Diversion header as TEL URI. > > > > > > > **diversion_inject_mode** (*enumerate\[string\]*): **none**, **did_number** > > > > *none* - Do not add Diversion header. > > > > *did_number* - Add Diversion header with DID number. > > > > > > > **cnam_lookup** (*boolean*) > > > > Enable CNAM (Caller Name) lookup for incoming calls. > > > > > > `}` > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **pop** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [POP Object](#pop-object) > > > > > **voice_in_trunk_group** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Voice In Trunk Group Object](#voice-in-trunk-group-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ##### SIP Trunk ``` json { "id": "f36d1d17-bd16-42b9-af42-0cfe166bf3ec", "type": "voice_in_trunks", "attributes": { "external_reference_id": null, "priority": 1, "capacity_limit": 10, "weight": 2, item.name: Voice OUT Trunk Object
item.description: #### Model > `{` > > > **type** (*string*): **voice_out_trunks** > > Trunks > > > **id** (*string*) > > Unique ID identifier of Trunk. > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > Trunk configuration complex object. > > > > > **name** (*string, NOT nullable*) > > > The voice out trunk name. > > > > > **allowed_rtp_ips** (*array[string], nullable*) > > > The allowed RTP IPs. Array from 0 to 10 items: IP v4/v6 single or subnet. > > > > > **on_cli_mismatch_action** (*enumerate[string], NOT nullable*): **send_original_cli**, **reject_call** > > > Possible values are: > > > *send_original_cli* - the system will pass the `FROM` header value to the termination gateway. > > > *reject_call* - the system will reject the call if the `FROM` header value does not match any of the allowed DIDs. > > > > > **capacity_limit** (*integer, nullable*): > > > The capacity limit of the voice out trunk. Allowed values from 0 to 32767. > > > > > **allow_any_did_as_cli** (*boolean, NOT nullable*): > > > When set to `True`, passed than DIDs relationship can not be set to explicit DIDs. if DIDs relationship is omitted in payload already linked DIDs will be automatically unassigned. > > > Default value is `False`. > > > > > **status** (*enumerate[string], NOT nullable*): **active**, **blocked** > > > Can be either: *active* or *blocked*. > > > Default value is `active`. > > > > > **threshold_amount** (*string, NOT nullable*): > > > The outbound trunk 24 hour threshold limit. Can be from 0.0 to 100000.0. > > > > > **default_dst_action** (*enumerate[string], NOT nullable*): **allow_all**, **reject_all** > > > Can be either: *allow_all* or *reject_all*. > > > Default value is `allow_all`. > > > > > **dst_prefixes** (*array[string], nullable*) > > > The allowed destination prefixes. > > > > > **media_encryption_mode** (*enumerate[string], NOT nullable*): **disabled**, **srtp_sdes**, **srtp_dtls**, **zrtp** > > > Media encryption mode. > > > See [RFC4568 about SRTP SDES](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc4568), [RFC5764 about SRTP DTLS](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5764), and [RFC6189 about ZRTP](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6189) for more details. > > > Possible values: > > > *disabled* - Disabled > > > *srtp_sdes* - SRTP SDES > > > *srtp_dtls* - SRTP DTLS > > > *zrtp* - ZRTP > > > > > **callback_url** (*string, nullable*) > > > Can be null or valid HTTP(s) URL. > > > > > **threshold_reached** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > The 24 hour limit indicator. If the threshold_amount has been reached during the 24 hours, this indicator will be `true`. > > > > > **force_symmetric_rtp** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > Forced to work in Symmetric RTP / COMEDIA mode. > > > > > **rtp_ping** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > Use RTP PING when connecting a call. > > > After establishing the call, DIDWW will send empty RTP packet RTP PING. > > > It is necessary if both parties operate in Symmetric RTP / Comedia mode and expect the other party to start sending RTP first. > > > > > **rtp_timeout** (*integer, nullable*) > > > RTP timeout in seconds. > > > > > **emergency_enable_all** (*boolean, NOT nullable*) > > > When set to `true`, all customer DIDs with emergency enabled are assigned to the trunk automatically. > > > > > **created_at** (*DateTime*) > > > Voice Out Trunk created at DateTime. > > > > > **authentication_method** (*object*) > > > Authentication method configuration. Contains `type` and `attributes`. > > > > > > > **type** (*enumerate\[string\]*): **ip_only**, **credentials_and_ip**, **twilio** > > > > Note: `ip_only` cannot be set on creation — only `credentials_and_ip` and `twilio` are allowed. `ip_only` is read-only once set. > > > > > > > **attributes** (*object*) — depends on `type`: > > > > > > > > For `ip_only` and `credentials_and_ip`: > > > > > > > > > **allowed_sip_ips** (*array\[string\], nullable*) > > > > > Allowed originating IPs. Array from 0 to 15 items: IPv4/IPv6 single or subnet. Read-only for `ip_only` after creation. > > > > > > > > > **tech_prefix** (*string, nullable*) > > > > > Technical prefix prepended to the destination number. Allowed only digits and `#`, max 8 characters. > > > > > > > > For `credentials_and_ip` only (read-only, auto-generated by server): > > > > > > > > > **username** (*string*) > > > > > Auto-generated SIP username. > > > > > > > > > **password** (*string*) > > > > > Auto-generated SIP password. > > > > > > > > For `twilio` only: > > > > > > > > > **twilio_account_sid** (*string*) > > > > > Twilio Account SID. > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string*) > > > Identifier for Voice OUT Trunk in external system. > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **dids** (*object*): ***to-many*** > > > relationship to [DID Object](#did-object) > > > The DID numbers if `allow_any_did_as_cli` is set to `false`. > > > > > **emergency_dids** (*object*): ***to-many*** > > > relationship to [DID Object](#did-object) > > > The DID numbers assigned for emergency calling. > > > > `}` > > > **meta** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **spent_amount** (*string*): > > > amount of spent according to `threshold_amount` > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ##### IP Only ``` json { "id": "457bf47d-446d-41cd-91c3-dfbda7bf0753", "type": "voice_out_trunks", "attributes": { "allowed_rtp_ips": ["0.0.0.0/0"], "allow_any_did_as_cli": true, "status": "active", "on_cli_mismatch_action": "send_original_cli", "name": "Outbound trunk 1", "capacity_limit": 100, "created_at": "2026-06-06T18:59:28.875Z", "threshold_reached": false, "threshold_amount": "10000.0", "media_encryption_mode": "disabled", "default_dst_action": "allow_all", "dst_prefixes": [], "force_symmetric_rtp": false, "rtp_ping": false, "rtp_timeout": 30, "emergency_enable_all": false, "callback_url": null, "external_reference_id": null, "authentication_method": { "type": "ip_only", "attributes": { "allowed_sip_ips": ["192.168.1.2/32"], "tech_prefix": null } } }, "relationships": { "dids": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/voice_out_trunks/457bf47d-446d-41cd-91c3-dfbda7bf0753/relationships/dids", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/voice_out_trunks/457bf47d-446d-41cd-91c3-dfbda7bf0753/dids" } }, "emergency_dids": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/voice_out_trunks/457bf47d-446d-41cd-91c3-dfbda7bf0753/relationships/emergency_dids", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/voice_out_trunks/457bf47d-446d-41cd-91c3-dfbda7bf0753/emergency_dids" } } }, "meta": { "spent_amount": "0.0" } } ``` ##### Credentials & IP-Based ``` json { "id": "457bf47d-446d-41cd-91c3-dfbda7bf0753", "type": "voice_out_trunks", "attributes": { "allowed_rtp_ips": ["0.0.0.0/0"], "allow_any_did_as_cli": true, "status": "active", "on_cli_mismatch_action": "send_original_cli", "name": "Outbound trunk 1", "capacity_limit": 100, "created_at": "2026-06-06T18:59:28.875Z", "threshold_reached": false, "threshold_amount": "10000.0", "media_encryption_mode": "disabled", "default_dst_action": "allow_all", "dst_prefixes": [], "force_symmetric_rtp": false, "rtp_ping": false, "rtp_timeout": 30, "emergency_enable_all": false, "callback_url": null, "external_reference_id": null, "authentication_method": { "type": "credentials_and_ip", "attributes": { "allowed_sip_ips": ["192.168.1.1/32"], "tech_prefix": null, "username": "7mfVbgdurFkORaf2", "password": "YKcRESEiq7BEMzHj" } } }, "relationships": { "dids": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/voice_out_trunks/457bf47d-446d-41cd-91c3-dfbda7bf0753/relationships/dids", "related": item.name: Capacity Pool Object
Channel Quantity Based Price Object
item.description: Quantity based pricing lets you automatically apply different discount prices to channels in Capacity pool that depend on the quantity. #### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **qty_based_pricings** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **setup_price** (*string*) > > > Price for Order creation per channel. > > > > > **monthly_price** (*string*) > > > Monthly price for Order renew based on Capacity pool billing cycle date (renew_date). > > > > > **qty** (*integer*) > > > Quantity on channels from which price per channel will be with discount. > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "id": "56dd9e31-a83a-472b-b3f7-158ef0755c31", "type": "qty_based_pricings", "attributes": { "qty": 10, "monthly_price": "4.0", "setup_price": "6.0" } } ```

#### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **capacity_pools** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **name** (*string*) > > > Name of the Capacity Pool. > > > > > **renew_date** (*date*) > > > Channel renewal date. > > > > > **total_channels_count** (*integer*) > > > Total number of channels in the Capacity Pool. > > > > > **assigned_channels_count** (*integer*) > > > Number of channels used in Shared Capacity Groups and/or assigned to DID numbers. > > > > > **minimum_limit_** (*integer*) > > > Minimum number of channels to be kept in the Capacity Pool. > > > > > **minimum_qty_per_order** (*integer*) > > > Minimum number of channels per Order. > > > > > **setup_price** (*string*) > > > Non Recurring Cost (one-time activation fee). > > > > > **monthly_price** (*string*) > > > Monthly Recurring Cost (ongoing monthly fee). > > > > > **metered_rate** (*string*) > > > Metered channel price per minute. > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **countries** (*object*): ***to-many*** > > > relationship to [Country Object](#country-object) > > > > > **shared_capacity_groups** (*object*): ***to-many*** > > > relationship to [Shared Capacity Group Object](#shared-capacity-group-object) > > > > > **qty_based_pricings** (*object*): ***to-many*** > > > relationship to [Quantity Based Price Object](#channel-quantity-based-price-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "id": "b8db1d7c-f415-4530-a340-c774bcc1c55f", "type": "capacity_pools", "attributes": { "name": "Extended", "renew_date": "2018-07-21", "total_channels_count": 10, "assigned_channels_count": 6, "minimum_limit": 5, "minimum_qty_per_order": 1, "setup_price": "25.0", "monthly_price": "25.0", "metered_rate": "0.02" }, "relationships": { "countries": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/capacity_pools/b8db1d7c-f415-4530-a340-c774bcc1c55f/relationships/countries", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/capacity_pools/b8db1d7c-f415-4530-a340-c774bcc1c55f/countries" } }, "shared_capacity_groups": { "links": { "self": item.name: Export Object
Export Filters Object
item.description:
#### Model

#### Inbound CDRs > `{` > > > **from** (*DateTime, required*) > > Start of the CDR timestamp range. > > > **to** (*DateTime, required*) > > End of the CDR timestamp range. > > > **did_number** (*string, optional*) > > Filters CDRs by DID number. > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "from": "2026-03-01 00:00:00", "to": "2026-03-31 23:59:59", "did_number": "123456789" } ```

#### Outbound CDRs > `{` > > > **from** (*DateTime, required*) > > Start of the CDR timestamp range. > > > **to** (*DateTime, required*) > > End of the CDR timestamp range. > > > **voice_out_trunk.id** (*string, optional*) > > Filters CDRs by Voice OUT Trunk ID. > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "from": "2026-03-01 00:00:00", "to": "2026-03-31 23:59:59", "voice_out_trunk.id": "457bf47d-446d-41cd-91c3-dfbda7bf0753" } ```

#### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **exports** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **status** (*enumerated\[string\], NOT nullable*): **pending**, **processing**, **completed** > > > Status can be "pending", "processing" or "completed". > > > > > **callback_url** (*string*): > > > The HTTP or HTTPS endpoint to where events related to export will be delivered. > > > > > **callback_method** (*string*): > > > The HTTP Method used for export events. **post**, **get** are supported methods. > > > > > **created_at** (*DateTime, NOT nullable*) > > > Timestamp when export request was created. > > > > > **url** (*string, nullable*) > > > The URL of csv file for downloading. Available only when status is "completed". > > > > > **export_type** (*enumerated[string], NOT nullable*): **cdr_in**, **cdr_out** > > > Defines CDR export type. > > > > > **filters** (*object*) > > > Export filters used when creating the export request. Required filters: `from`, `to`. Optional filters: `did_number` (for `cdr_in`), `voice_out_trunk.id` (for `cdr_out`). > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string*) > > > Identifier for Export in external system. > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "data": { "id": "9cccd228-2c1a-435f-b7eb-77ce6fc00a86", "type": "exports", "attributes": { "status": "pending", "created_at": "2017-06-25T14:56:31.513Z", "url": null, "callback_url": null, "callback_method": null, "export_type": "cdr_in", "filters": { "from": "2026-03-01 00:00:00", "to": "2026-03-31 23:59:59", "did_number": "123456789" }, "external_reference_id": null } } } ```

item.name: Address Verification Object
item.description: #### Model > `{` > > > **id** (*string*) > > > **type** (*string*): **address_verifications** > > > **attributes** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **external_reference_id** (*string*) > > > Identifier for Address Verification in external system. > > > > > **service_description** (*string*) > > > The description of verification task. > > > > > **callback_url** (*string*) > > > The HTTP or HTTPS endpoint to where events related to verification task will be delivered. > > > > > **callback_method** (*string*) > > > The HTTP Method used for verification task events. post, get are supported methods. > > > > > **status** (*enumerated\[string\]*): **pending**, **approved**, **rejected** > > > The status of verification task. > > > > > **reject_reasons** (*array\[string\]*) > > > The reasons for verification rejection. Empty array when not rejected. > > > > > **reject_comment** (*string, nullable*) > > > Additional comment provided upon rejection. > > > > > **reference** (string) > > > Address verification reference number. > > > > > **created_at** (*DateTime\[string\]*) > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **dids** (*object*): ***to-many*** > > > relationship to [DID Object](#did-object) > > > > > **address** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Address Object](#address-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` Example: ``` json { "id": "89c0164e-752f-4995-a0fa-0ced21e60e4a", "type": "address_verifications", "attributes": { "external_reference_id": null, "service_description": "string", "callback_url": "http://10.201.10.1/test.php", "callback_method": "get", "status": "pending", "reject_reasons": [], "reject_comment": null, "reference": "SHB-485120", "created_at": "2021-03-23T12:20:04.584Z" }, "relationships": { "dids": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/address_verifications/89c0164e-752f-4995-a0fa-0ced21e60e4a/relationships/dids", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/address_verifications/89c0164e-752f-4995-a0fa-0ced21e60e4a/dids" } }, "address": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/address_verifications/89c0164e-752f-4995-a0fa-0ced21e60e4a/relationships/address", "related": "https://api.didww.com/v3/address_verifications/89c0164e-752f-4995-a0fa-0ced21e60e4a/address" } } } } ```

level for Personal Identity. > > > > > **business_area_level** (*enumerate\[string\]*): **world_wide**, **country** > > > Required location proof level for Business Identity. > > > > > **address_mandatory_fields** (*array\[string\]*) > > > Mandatory fields for Address. > > > > > **personal_mandatory_fields** (*array\[string\]*) > > > Mandatory fields for Personal Identity. > > > > > **business_mandatory_fields** (*array\[string\]*) > > > Mandatory fields for Business Identity. > > > > > **estimate_setup_time** (*string*) > > > Estimated time to activate emergency calling service. > > > > > **requirement_restriction_message** (*string, nullable*) > > > Additional restriction message for specific Country/Area. > > > > `}` > > > **meta** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **setup_price** (*string*) > > > One-time setup price for the emergency calling service. > > > > > **monthly_price** (*string*) > > > Monthly price for the emergency calling service. > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **country** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Country Object](#country-object) > > > > > **did_group_type** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [DID Group Type Object](#did-group-type-object) > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "id": "3e353579-8056-4b9b-bf83-ea57885bee32", "type": "emergency_requirements", "attributes": { "identity_type": "personal", "address_area_level": "city", "personal_area_level": "country", "business_area_level": null, "address_mandatory_fields": [ "State/Province/Region" ], "personal_mandatory_fields": [ "Birth Date", "Contact email" ], "business_mandatory_fields": [], "estimate_setup_time": "1-2 business days", "requirement_restriction_message": null }, "relationships": { "country": { "links": { "self": "https://api.didww.com/v3/emergency_requirements/3e353579-8056-4b9b-bf83-ea57885bee32/relationships/country", "related": HTTPS endpoint to where events related to verification will be delivered. > > > > > **callback_method** (*string, nullable*) > > > The HTTP Method used for verification events. post, get are supported Verification in external system. > > > > `}` > > > **relationships** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **address** (*object*): ***to-one*** > > > relationship to [Address Object](#address-object) > > > > > item.name: Errors
Validation Error Object
item.description: JSONAPI validation error. #### Model > `{` > > > **title** (*string*) > > Short error description. > > > **detail** (*string*) > > Detailed error description. > > > **code** (*string*) > > Will be `100`. > > > **status** (*string*) > > Response status code > > > **source** (*object*) `{` > > > > > **pointer** (*string*) > > > Path to error attribute/relationship in request payload. > > > > `}` > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "title": "has already been taken", "detail": "name - has already been taken", "code": "100", "source": { "pointer": "/data/attributes/name" }, "status": "422" } ```

Will be `400`. > > `}` #### Example ``` json { "title": "Insufficient funds", "detail": "Insufficient funds", "code": "400", "status": "400" } ```

item.name: Callbacks Details
item.description: Callbacks allow you to receive events related to your [Orders](#orders), [Exports](#export), [Address Verifications](#address-verifications) and [Voice out trunks](#voice-out-trunks) via HTTP request. Configure a *callback_url* and *callback_method* attributes when these objects using the REST API, and DIDWW will make an HTTP request (webhook) to that URL whenever an event takes place for them. *callback_url* can be set to any valid URL. *callback_method* can be either *GET* or *POST*. With *GET* request you will receive payload as query parameters. *POST* request will set the “Content-Type” header to “application/x-www-form-urlencoded” with body formatted according to content type. ## Order Callback Request Parameters In case of order status change DIDWW makes an HTTP request to the *callback_url* you’ve set with following parameters: | Parameter | Description | | --- | --- | | id | ID of an order | | type | ‘orders’ | | status | ‘completed’ or ‘canceled’ | ## Export Callback Request Parameters In case of export complete DIDWW makes an HTTP request to the *callback_url* you’ve set with following parameters: | Parameter | Description | | --- | --- | | id | ID of an export | | type | ‘exports’ | | status | ‘completed’ | | url (see Note) | Download URL of the exported .csv.gz file | > **Note** > > For **CDR In** (`cdr_in`) and **CDR Out** (`cdr_out`) export types created via API v3 2026-06-06, the callback payload additionally includes a `url` parameter with a download link to the exported file. ## Address Verification Callback Request Parameters In case of address verification status change DIDWW makes an HTTP request to the *callback_url* you’ve set with following parameters: | Parameter | Description | | --- | --- | | id | ID of an address verification | | type | ‘address_verifications’ | | status | ‘approved’ or ‘rejected’ | | reject_reason | only for rejected status | | reject_comment | string with explanation (only for rejected status) | ## Emergency Verification Callback Request Parameters In case of emergency verification status change DIDWW makes an HTTP request to the *callback_url* you’ve set with following parameters: | Parameter | Description | | --- | --- | | id | ID of an emergency verification | | type | ‘emergency_verifications’ | | status | ‘approved’ or ‘rejected’ | | reject_reasons | rejection reasons string; empty string when approved | | reject_comment | string with explanation (only for rejected status) | | emergency_calling_service_id | ID of the related Emergency Calling Service | > **Note** > > When Emergency Calling Service is activated, an order is created and the Order Callback is triggered with the same parameters (id, type, status). ## Voice OUT Trunk Callback Request Parameters Configure a *callback_url* attribute for single resource via the REST API, and DIDWW will make an HTTP POST request (webhook) to that URL whenever an event takes place for it. *callback_url* can be set to any valid URL. In case of Voice OUT Trunk being blocked due to set 24 hour limit value being reached or trunk being unblocked DIDWW makes an HTTP POST request to the *callback_url* with “Content-Type” header set to “application/json” and body as JSON array with one or more JSON objects. Each JSON object will have following parameters: | Parameter | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | id | string | ID of a Voice OUT Trunk | | type | string | ‘voice_out_trunks’ | | status | string | ‘active’ or ‘blocked’ | | threshold_reached | boolean | false or true | | created_at | string | Date and Time of event creation | **Example** ``` json [ { "id": "f36d1d17-bd16-42b9-af42-0cfe166bf3ec", "type": "voice_out_trunks", "status": "blocked", "threshold_reached": true, "created_at": "2017-06-25T08:21:41.795Z" } ] ``` ## HTTP Request Validation Each HTTP request is signed with an **X-DIDWW-Signature** HTTP header. DIDWW uses the parameters sent in the webhook and the exact URL your application supplied to DIDWW to create this signature. The signature uses the HMAC-SHA1 hashing algorithm with your DIDWW account’s **API Key** as the secret key. Your application can verify that this signature is correct using the server side DIDWW SDKs: > * [didww-v3](https://github.com/didww/didww-v3-ruby/blob/master/lib/didww/callback/request_validator.rb) Ruby gem > * [didww/didww-api-3-php-sdk](https://github.com/didww/didww-api-3-php-sdk/blob/master/src/Callback/RequestValidator.php) PHP library > * [didww-api-3-java-sdk](https://github.com/didww/didww-api-3-java-sdk/blob/main/src/main/java/com/didww/sdk/callback/RequestValidator.java) Java SDK > * [didww-api-3-python-sdk](https://github.com/didww/didww-api-3-python-sdk/blob/main/src/didww/callback/request_validator.py) Python SDK > * [didww-api-3-typescript-sdk](https://github.com/didww/didww-api-3-typescript-sdk/blob/main/src/callback/request-validator.ts) TypeScript SDK > * [didww-api-3-go-sdk](https://github.com/didww/didww-api-3-go-sdk/blob/main/callback.go) Go SDK > * [didww-api-3-dotnet-sdk](https://github.com/didww/didww-api-3-dotnet-sdk/blob/main/src/Didww.Api3/Callback/RequestValidator.cs) .NET SDK You will need your account’s **API Key**, the value of the **X-DIDWW-Signature** HTTP header that DIDWW passed to you, the URL that DIDWW sent the webhook to, and all of the parameters sent by DIDWW. ## IP addresses Requests are being sent from IPv4 network 46.19.208.0/21 and IPv6 network 2a01:ad00::/32 ## HTTP Response Error Handling When DIDWW receives **non 2XX** response status code from an HTTP request to the *callback_url* it will re-attempt to send it again up to 9 times. After 10 attempt DIDWW will stop sending callback event. HTTP request timeout more than 60 seconds will consider as failed response. | Callback attempt | Wait interval | | --- | --- | | 2 | 1 minute | | 3 | 10 minutes | | 4 | 30 minutes | | 5 | 1 hour | | 6 | 3 hours | | 7 | 6 hours | | 8 | 12 hours | | 9 | 1 day | | 10 | 2 days | ## Testing Callbacks on local machine behind NAT In order to test Callbacks feature on local servers behind NAT, tools such as [ngrock](https://ngrok.com/docs) or [localtunnel](https://theboroer.github.io/localtunnel-www/) could be used. These tools allows you to expose a web server running on your local machine to the internet.
